title: Most Emotional Scenes in Rebuild of Evangelion
display_title: Most Emotional Scenes in Rebuild of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Most Emotional Scenes in Rebuild of Evangelion?             Quote Post by movieartman » Tue Apr 08, 2014 6:41 pm WHAT IN YOUR PERSONAL EXPERIENCE IS THE MOST EMOTIONAL SCENES IN REBUILD OF EVANGELION? Ok we have a emotional scene thread - http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11265/Whats-the-most-emotional-scene-in-NGE/? But looking it over pretty much no one has talked about such scenes from rebuild yet. So here is its counterpart just for the rebuild movies. My personal pics 1.) EVA 2.0 YOU CAN NOT ADVANCE ---- From when Eva Unit 2 Pulls Shinji out of the Bunker all the way thru to The End of the End Credits Scene and Preview. this scene is just far beyond expectations, #2 was my number one when i first watched 3.0 but upon rewatching this, its almost definitely #1 there is just SOOO many layers and varied emotions to this sequence, its genuinely amazing. __Shinji in unit2's palm just sees the destruction that has occured thanks to him not piloting anymore, SEE THIS SHIT BOY!__ __Zeruel abruptly releases the giant tounge and suddenly unceremoniously devours Unit0 & Rei, its so out of the blue im just like, WTF was that!__ __The Thantos Theme starts playing, cue memories of Rei's Death in the original series and it really starts to sink in__ __Shinji jumps down and starts running thru the wasteland, and im all like YES GET YOUR ASS MOVING, FIX THIS SHIT NOW!__ __Shinji confronts Gendo and demands to pilot unit1 again, gendo's reaction is much more surprised here than in the original series (if memory serves me correctly), all the better for it__ __Zeruel blast off unit1's arm, the blood splatters across gendo's unwavering face, i just can not read his expression, could be anything from "this shit is going to end fucking horribly" to "fuck yeah my boy has become a man" to something as simple as "This was NOT the Plan!"__ __Unit1 looses all power, we all knew what was coming__ __"I WANT Rei, GIVE HER BACK!" oh Fuck!, the reckoning has arrived bitch!"__ __The Curbstomp begins, a awesome metal influenced version of the beast 2 theme plays, Laser replacment arm FUCK YES!, SO MUCH WIN!__ __the halo appears above unit1's head, ok thats new...........? (uneasy feeling in pit of stomach surges)__ __"Shinji PLEASE STOP, YOU WON'T BE ABLE TO COME BACK"....... oh no__ __"I Dont care what happens to me, I Dont even care what happens to the world, but im bringing Rei Back" Dude you are seriously damaging my composure!__ __"YOU CAN DO IT Shinji, DO IT FOR YOURSELF, DO IT BECAUSE THIS IS WHAT YOU WANT" hopefulness briefly gabs me again.... for the last time__ __"ITS NO USE"........... please no!__ __"IM SAVING YOU RIGHT NOW!" [3rd impact waves spread around him, give me wings starts], dear god no, they can't, THEY CAN NOT BE DOING THIS NOW!__ __"we are almost finished" F... U... C... K... Y... O... U... G... E... N... D... O..._ __"THIS IS ALL I NEED" look up Bitter fucking Sweet in the dictionary, you will find a video link to this shot__ __At this point the background music for give me wings has become so beautiful it has surpassed the Early qualm i had with the kid like voice__ __Tokyo 3 above ground begins disintegrating --- "the toll of its creation, is the demise of all life that came before it" nononononononononono__ __"Wings, Ive seen them before!" im so fucking sorry for Misato!__ __"This is the End of the World!" Cuts to Black! NOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO__ __lays there broken listening to beautiful world in the credits__ __spear flys down shatters the 3rd impact wave. THANK YOU GOD!, You Monsters!, this is not a marvel film, do you have any idea how many fucking people walked out of the theaters after "this is the end of the world" who will never forgive you?__ SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Most Emotional Scenes in Rebuild of Evangelion?             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Apr 08, 2014 6:47 pm For me, probably Kaworu's death.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Apr 08, 2014 8:26 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Apr 08, 2014 9:22 pm 3.0. Which moment in it? Just 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue Apr 08, 2014 9:52 pm Every time Shinji loses his s__t I get emotional.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 08, 2014 9:58 pm Kaworu's death, the scene where Shinji goes up against Sahaquiel, when Eva-01 bites Asuka's plug, when Shinji is dragging himself back to the positron rifle, and when Shinji goes all shiny-arm on Zeruel. Honorable mention for the end of 3.33 when Shinji and co. are walking off into the red desert. It moved me- I don't know why.

[]:             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Tue Apr 08, 2014 10:08 pm The ending of 2.22 and also the battle between Unit 01 and unit 03(Bardiel).

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Apr 08, 2014 10:15 pm Ray wrote: 3.0. Which moment in it? Just 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by movieartman » Tue Apr 08, 2014 11:56 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Kaworu's death, the scene where Shinji goes up against Sahaquiel, when Eva-01 bites Asuka's plug, when Shinji is dragging himself back to the positron rifle, and when Shinji goes all shiny-arm on Zeruel. Honorable mention for the end of 3.33 when Shinji and co. are walking off into the red desert. It moved me- I don't know why.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 08, 2014 11:58 pm movieartman wrote: Oh your not alone, the dessert trek was very powerful.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 09, 2014 12:52 am NGE is an emotional story, and NME is all that emotion condensed into film format; the entire set of films has been an emotional ride and has been escalating as it's progressed. Q was a very high point in intense, confused and anxious passion, but 2.22 also did a great job presenting whimsical, heroic and even romantic emotions. Hell, 1.11 even had powerful nostalgic emotion with the new shiny animation; don't even get me started on Ramiel and Operation Yashima. The point is, Evangelion offers a wide array of emotions, each with their own high points. I guess the real question is: which is your favorite emotion to feel? I personally get-off on that dark, depressing shit Q had to offer, yet at the same time it also provided feelings of mystery, awe, and wonder; and the use of colors really adds another touch to experience. Overall, I think Q did the best at playing with the audience's emotions, and that's why it's my favorite. If I had to pick a particular moment in the film, I would say it would be immediately following Fuyu's info dump. The music that plays is very dark and ominous, and you can really sense Shinji's feelings of betrayal and building anger; and then when he confronts Rei and pretty much confirms everything Fuyutsuki said and that simply broke the poor kid. His walk thereafter with the swaying animation and the repeating voices was quite an intense expression of overwhelming anxiety and panic; and then suddenly silence. The next cut is him absolutely alone and at his worst. His SDAT no longer comforts him, probably because it now reminds him of Rei, so he casts it off in his anger. The world is a very dismal place for Shinji at this moment. I found this segment of the movie much more emotionally touching than even Kaworu's death. Maybe that's just because I never trusted him; his lessons are good enough, but his promise of hope was bullshit and it backfired on him and it shattered Shinji even further, if not completely. I think 4.0 will show us the promise of Shinji finding a realistic form of hope (ie, not something that involves trying to change the world with magical powers). Shinji will also likely be around people now and not absolutely alone; he'll at least be with Asuka and Rei for a bit in the beginning. I think the feelings of intense isolation that were the focus of those scenes I mentioned previously will not be presented as powerfully in 4.0. Hopefully we've ridden out that storm and the next step is recovering from the emotional intensity of Q.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Wed Apr 09, 2014 1:16 am Ray wrote: 3.0. Which moment in it? Just 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Wed Apr 09, 2014 1:19 am AngelNo13Bardiel wrote: Gonna have to go with this, too. Also... This gets a very close second place. I got punched right in the feels.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Apr 09, 2014 9:17 am When the dummy system takes control of Unit 01 and crushes Unit-03's entry plug in 2.0

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Apr 09, 2014 12:29 pm Dataprime wrote: When the dummy system takes control of Unit 01 and crushes Unit-03's entry plug in 2.0

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Apr 09, 2014 12:39 pm Ray wrote: I feel that was handled much better in the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Apr 09, 2014 4:11 pm Personally I think that making Shinji not seeing what was happening was even worse for him : at least with Toji he could immediately assess the damages done to him, but with Asuka he could only guess what happened, and the only indication he got was the sound of grinding metal from EVA-03's entry plug and Asuka's agonizing scream! Also the music is an horrible tearjerker, not only because of the dissonance, but because the lyrics are actually a foreshadowing of the fact they won't see of each other for the next 14 years (at least from Asuka's POV) : Let's be friends Forever, without end Tomorrow, we'll dream About the path of hope Like the birds winging through the sky We will live freely So long, farewell for today Until the day we meet again So long, farewell for today Until the day we meet again Let's treasure The joy of trusting each other So long, farewell for today Until the day we meet again Until the day we meet again

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Apr 09, 2014 4:13 pm Well. . . I watched the movie dubbed and w/o subtitles, and I don't speak Japanese, so I guess it was lost on me. I hope they can be friends again in Final. . . but I'm crossing my fingers, and bracing for Final Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Apr 09, 2014 4:48 pm Having that thing in front of his face and having to squirm around in the seat just made it worse.

 and ominous, and you can really sense Shinji's feelings of betrayal and building anger; and then when he confronts Rei and pretty much confirms everything Fuyutsuki said and that simply broke the poor kid. His walk thereafter with the swaying animation and the repeating voices was quite an intense expression of overwhelming anxiety and panic; and then suddenly silence.
